name: BP P.L.C. v. Mayor and City Council of Baltimore
question: Does federal law permit a court of appeals to review any issue included in a district court’s order remanding a case to state court, or only the ground for removal?
description: A case in which the Court held that federal law permits a court of appeals to review any issue included in a district court’s order sending a case to state court when the move to state court is based on two statutes, not just the ground for removal itself.
